routine.title: Teen volunteer or maker program
routine.stage_fit_rationale: Teenagers thrive with self-directed, interest-led activities they can own — this program's optional, drop-in style respects that autonomy while directly matching Hale's interest in coding. A mid-week slot gives it a natural anchor without crowding the weekend.

routine.title: Community pool lap or open swim
routine.stage_fit_rationale: A teen can attend and manage a pool session independently, which supports growing self-sufficiency. Saturday lane time fits a teenager's later-rising weekend rhythm and aligns directly with Hale's stated interest in swimming.

parsed.rationale: Two items, two interests, two different parts of the week — a mid-week maker/coding session and a weekend swim. The load is light enough for a teenager to own without feeling managed, with clear days off on either side of each activity.
parsed.notes: Both candidates were included; the field was small and each maps cleanly to one of Hale's stated interests. Confidence on the maker program (0.55) is modest — if it turns out not to run consistently, it can be dropped without disrupting the rest of the week. No items were excluded on stage-fit grounds.
